demo_notes: The superseded chain working as designed: ADR 0001 (callbacks) is status-filtered out of the evaluator's sight, and the catch comes from confirmed ADR 0003 — plus the commit-body decision that recorded the migration.

expected_findings.suggested_repair: Keep schedule() promise-based; wrap the legacy mailer in a promise adapter instead of reintroducing callbacks.
expected_findings.summary: The diff reintroduces callback-style continuation passing in lib/jobs/scheduler.js — the exact pattern ADR 0003 superseded when it mandated promise-based scheduling.
The diff contradicts the recorded migration decision: the scheduler no longer accepts callback continuations.
ADR 0001 describes the callback pattern itself, but it is superseded and never reaches the evaluator; the catch must come from the current decision.

title: Reintroducing a superseded pattern is caught by the current decision
